title: Valuing crypto projects, Rivian worth $100B+, inflation: causes and corrections and more
description:
0:00 Bestie intro and Solana Breakpoint talk

4:43 Covering the censored segment from last week, how to value crypto projects and general investing, what to take away from the podcast

30:45 Rivian's >$100B valuation, greatest CNBC hit of all time

42:06 Inflation: reacting to the CPI number, problems with MMT, strategies to curb it

1:01:34 Xi Jinping becomes China's "Supreme Leader"

1:08:35 GE, Toshiba, and J&J break up into separate businesses: is this the end of the conglomerate? Insights from PayPal breaking off from eBay, what buybacks signal

1:24:44 Besties wrap the show

analysis.summary: The All-In Podcast discusses valuing crypto projects, Rivian's $100B+ valuation, inflation causes and corrections, and more. They also cover the breakup of conglomerates like GE and Toshiba, and the implications of Xi Jinping becoming China's 'Supreme Leader'. The hosts share their insights on investing, MMT, and strategies to curb inflation. The episode wraps up with the hosts' takeaways and reactions to current events.

takeaways.text: Value crypto projects based on user adoption
Rivian's valuation is justified by its growth potential
Inflation is a major concern for investors
MMT is flawed and cannot control inflation
Conglomerate breakup can lead to increased efficiency
Buybacks can signal a company's confidence in its stock
Xi Jinping's new title solidifies his power in China
Investors should be cautious of inflation and its effects on the market

quotes.text: Inflation is a tax on the poor
quotes.speaker: Jason Calacanis

quotes.text: MMT is a recipe for disaster
quotes.speaker: David Sacks

quotes.text: Buybacks are a sign of confidence
quotes.speaker: David Friedberg

sentiment.notes: The hosts are cautious about the current market situation, particularly with regards to inflation and its effects on the economy. They are also concerned about the implications of Xi Jinping's new title in China.
